questions.question: Explain if/else vs switch. When would you use each?
questions.answer: If/else checks arbitrary boolean conditions and supports ranges. Switch compares one value against discrete cases. Use if/else for complex conditions; switch for single-value multi-case comparisons, which is often faster (jump table) and more readable.
questions.followUp: How would you refactor a long if/else chain?

questions.question: What is short-circuit evaluation?
questions.answer: Logical operators &&/|| stop evaluating once the result is determined. In "A && B", if A is false, B is never evaluated — useful for guarding: `if (user && user.name)`. In "A || B", if A is true, B is skipped — useful for defaults: `const name = input || "default"`.

questions.question: How do you test all branches of a complex conditional without exponential test cases?
questions.answer: Use equivalence partitioning and boundary value analysis. Group inputs into classes. Test one example per class plus boundaries. For "score >= 90 -> A, score >= 80 -> B", test 89, 90, 79, 80.
